title: Remote Assistance Software Comparison 2026: Top Solutions Review
description: Compare leading remote assistance software in 2026. Review features, ease of use, security, and pricing for TeamViewer, AnyDesk, ConnectWise, and alternatives.
content:
Remote Assistance Software Landscape 2026

Remote assistance software enables support technicians to provide hands-on help to customers through remote access, screen sharing, and guided collaboration tools. In 2026, this software category encompasses diverse approaches from full remote desktop control to cobrowsing assistance, each suited to different support scenarios.

Organizations choosing remote assistance software must consider support team expertise, customer technical literacy, security requirements, and budget constraints. The best solution balances technician capabilities with customer comfort, providing effective assistance without creating friction or security concerns.

Remote Assistance Solutions Comparison

Our comparison evaluates platforms across the remote assistance spectrum, from full control solutions to guided assistance tools. Key criteria include customer connection ease, assistance depth, security implementation, and total cost.

Types of Remote Assistance

Understanding assistance type distinctions helps organizations select appropriate solutions for specific support scenarios. Different approaches suit different customer needs and security requirements.

  • Full Remote Control: Technicians take complete control of customer devices, enabling direct problem resolution. This approach minimizes customer involvement but requires trust and appropriate security measures.
  • Guided Assistance: Technicians view customer screens and provide verbal or visual guidance without direct control. Customers perform actions based on technician instructions, maintaining autonomy while receiving expert help.
  • Cobrowsing: Technicians navigate alongside customers within web applications, highlighting elements and suggesting actions. This approach suits web-centric support scenarios without full system access concerns.
  • AR-Assisted Support: Augmented reality tools allow customers to share camera views while technicians overlay guidance on physical objects. This emerging approach suits hardware troubleshooting and field service scenarios.
  • Annotation-Based Help: Drawing and highlighting tools direct customer attention to interface elements. Technicians mark screens visually while verbally explaining required actions.

TeamViewer Assist AR: Innovative Approach

TeamViewer Assist AR (formerly TeamViewer Pilot) introduces augmented reality to remote assistance, enabling customers to share smartphone camera views while technicians overlay digital annotations on real-world objects. This approach suits hardware troubleshooting, equipment setup, and field service support.

Technicians see live video from customer cameras and can place 3D markers, arrows, and text overlays on physical objects through AR technology. Customers follow visual guidance to perform physical actions, maintaining control while receiving precise expert direction.

The technology excels for equipment maintenance, product assembly assistance, and hardware diagnostic scenarios where traditional screen-based remote access cannot help. Pricing at $59 monthly reflects the specialized AR capabilities. Organizations with hardware support needs find this approach invaluable.

ConnectWise Control: Professional Control Solution

ConnectWise Control provides comprehensive remote control capabilities with excellent customization options for support workflows. The platform supports both full control assistance and annotation-based guidance, allowing technicians to adjust approaches based on customer preference and issue complexity.

Advanced toolbox features enable running diagnostic scripts and deploying utilities during sessions, automating repetitive procedures. Browser-based connections eliminate software installation requirements, reducing customer effort at session start.

Customizable branding and portal creation allow organizations to present consistent identity during support interactions. Integration with ConnectWise PSA maintains workflow continuity for MSPs. At $27 monthly, ConnectWise Control delivers exceptional value for professional support operations.

Surfly: Pure Cobrowsing Platform

Surfly specializes entirely in cobrowsing assistance, enabling technicians to navigate alongside customers within web applications without any remote control. This approach addresses security concerns while providing effective guidance for web-based support scenarios.

Browser-based connection requires zero customer installation. Technicians see exactly what customers see in web pages, can highlight elements, and suggest navigation paths. Customer actions remain under customer control, addressing compliance requirements in regulated industries.

Surfly excels for e-commerce support, banking assistance, SaaS application help, and any scenario focused on web interfaces. Pricing at $59 monthly reflects specialized cobrowsing focus. Organizations should evaluate whether pure web assistance matches their support needs before selecting Surfly.

Cobrowse.io: Enterprise Web Assistance

Cobrowse.io targets enterprise organizations requiring web-based assistance with advanced security and compliance features. The platform enables cobrowsing without customer software installation while maintaining detailed audit trails for regulatory compliance.

Technicians can view and highlight web page elements, guide navigation, and assist form completion without accessing customer credentials or personal data. This selective visibility addresses privacy concerns while enabling effective web support.

Integration with enterprise CRM and ticketing systems maintains workflow continuity. Cobrowse.io complies with GDPR and other privacy regulations, suiting financial services, healthcare, and government support operations. Pricing at $49 monthly provides good enterprise value.

Choosing Assistance Type for Support Scenarios

Selecting between full control, guided assistance, and cobrowsing depends on issue type, customer preference, and security requirements. Organizations often benefit from multiple approaches for different scenarios.

  • Technical Issues: Complex system configuration, software installation, and troubleshooting benefit from full remote control where technicians can directly resolve issues. Customer technical literacy matters less when technicians control sessions.
  • Process Guidance: Helping customers navigate interfaces, complete forms, or understand features works well with annotation-based guidance. Customers learn while receiving help, reducing future support needs for similar issues.
  • Web Application Support: Cobrowsing suits scenarios focused entirely on web interfaces. Banking, e-commerce, and SaaS support teams benefit from web-specific tools without full system access concerns.
  • Hardware Assistance: AR-assisted support addresses physical equipment issues that screen-based tools cannot help. Field service, manufacturing, and consumer electronics support benefit from camera-sharing with AR overlays.

Security Considerations by Assistance Type

Security requirements vary significantly across assistance approaches. Organizations must evaluate risk levels and implement appropriate safeguards for each assistance type deployed.

  • Full Control Risks: Technicians with complete system access can view all files, modify configurations, and install software. Strict access policies, session recording, and permission restrictions mitigate risks.
  • Guided Assistance Risks: Viewing customer screens reveals potentially sensitive information without control capability. Privacy concerns remain relevant though manipulation risks are lower.
  • Cobrowsing Security: Web-focused cobrowsing reduces risk by limiting visibility to browser content. Advanced platforms can exclude sensitive fields like password entries and financial data from technician view.
  • AR Session Privacy: Camera sharing reveals customer physical environment. Technicians may see personal spaces, equipment, and surroundings. Clear consent and session limits address privacy concerns.

Implementation Best Practices

Deploying remote assistance software requires clear policies, technician training, and customer communication strategies. Organizations should establish governance before enabling any remote assistance capabilities.

  • Document Consent Procedures: Ensure customers explicitly approve remote assistance before sessions begin. Document consent for compliance records and customer protection.
  • Train on Assistance Approaches: Technicians should understand when to use full control versus guidance versus cobrowsing. Training should cover communication techniques for each assistance type.
  • Configure Security Features: Enable encryption, authentication, and audit features appropriate to each assistance type. Default configurations may not meet organizational security standards.
  • Establish Session Limits: Define maximum session duration, timeout policies, and access scope restrictions. Limits protect customers and reduce organizational risk exposure.

Platform Selection Summary

Organizations should match assistance type to support scenario requirements. Full control platforms like ConnectWise Control and AnyDesk suit technical troubleshooting. Cobrowsing solutions like Surfly and Cobrowse.io address web-focused support with lower security risk. AR assistance like TeamViewer Assist AR enables hardware support impossible with screen-based tools.

Budget considerations favor ConnectWise Control for comprehensive value, while specialized approaches justify higher costs for specific scenario benefits. Organizations with diverse support needs may require multiple platforms for different assistance types.
author: Remote Support Specialist

pros: Multiple assistance types address diverse scenarios
cons: Specialized tools require multiple platform investments
AR adoption limited by hardware requirements
Customer preference varies by assistance type

faq.question: What assistance type works best for low-technical-literacy customers?
faq.answer: Full remote control works best for customers with limited technical skills, as technicians can resolve issues directly without requiring customer action understanding. However, customer comfort with remote control should be assessed before proceeding.

faq.question: How does cobrowsing protect customer privacy?
faq.answer: Advanced cobrowsing platforms can mask sensitive fields like passwords, credit card numbers, and personal identifiers from technician view. Technicians see web navigation and interface elements without accessing private data entered in forms.

faq.question: What hardware is required for AR-assisted remote support?
faq.answer: AR-assisted support requires customers to have smartphones with cameras and AR capabilities (most modern iOS and Android devices). Technicians need standard computers with the AR support platform software. No special AR glasses or equipment is required.
